summary: Well‑balanced soundboard captures a solid 1991 show with standout Sugaree and Stranger, though PA hiccups cut the first set short.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced SBD that lets the band’s 1991 energy shine – especially on Sugaree, Stranger, New Speedway and Wharf Rat.  The concert was marred by a PA outage after Masterpiece that shortened the first set and caused some right‑channel distortion, and the second set was viewed as uneven, but overall fans remember a good vibe and strong playing.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the soundboard quality and highlight several songs, while a few note serious PA/recording glitches and an uneven second set; the balance of positive vs. negative points yields an overall four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp, well‑balanced soundboard with excellent keyboard clarity; occasional right‑channel wince and short‑circuit noise from the PA outage during Masterpiece.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set was cut short after Masterpiece due to a PA failure, causing the band to shorten the set and miss Bird Song.  Rain‑related technical issues added noise at the end of the first set.  The second set received mixed reactions – some felt it was not well played, though individual moments (Stranger intro) were praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned after the show that the band "had a little life to it"; no specific guitar critique.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's early notes on Stranger were highlighted as crystal‑clear; post‑Brent keyboard work (Wharf Rat, Sugaree) praised.
